source.title: Effective Diversity in Population Based Reinforcement Learning

confidence_rationale: NeurIPS 2020 spotlight. Introduces DvD (Diversity via Determinants), which measures population diversity as the volume of a behavioral embedding manifold via the log-determinant of the kernel matrix. Critical finding: traditional pairwise diversity methods suffer from cycling and redundancy -- only volume-based measures prevent degeneracy. Mapping fidelity is high for ARC-065 (what 'effective' diversity means operationally). Caveat: operates on a population of agents, not a single policy with context variable; direct translation to REE's single-agent CEM requires adaptation.
failure_signatures: Pairwise distance-based diversity measures (e.g. mean L2 distance between policies) suffer from cycling: a population can have high mean pairwise distance but still cluster into a few degenerate modes. Volume-based measures are necessary to catch this failure mode.

Explicitly boosting diversity often hurts performance when reward-landscape exploration is not needed -- REE should not apply diversity pressure uniformly throughout training but adaptively (DvD uses online learning to modulate diversity weight).

Behavioral embeddings that are domain-specific or handcrafted introduce an inductive bias that shapes which diversity modes are captured; REE's embedding must be tuned to capture route-mode diversity, not just action diversity.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Diversity in a population-based RL system should be measured as the volume of the population in behavioral space (log-det of the pairwise kernel matrix), not as pairwise distances. Volume measurement via DvD prevents degeneracy (cycling, mode collapse to a few clusters) that pairwise methods miss. Diversity pressure should be adaptive, not fixed.
mapping.ree_translation: ARC-065 claims behavioral diversity generation requires structured mechanisms beyond noise (MECH-313 = structured noise/diversity floor). DvD's volume-based metric suggests that measuring 'how many distinct route modes are active in REE's candidate population' requires a determinant-like measure over the trajectory embedding space, not just mean pairwise trajectory distance. For REE: compute diversity of CEM candidate trajectories via their behavioral descriptor embeddings, use log-det as the diversity signal. This would let the diversity pressure adapt -- high when candidates are degenerate, low when already diverse.
mapping.mapping_caveat: DvD operates across a population of independently trained agents; REE generates diversity within a single CEM candidate set at inference time. The formal connection is the same (avoid degenerate pairwise distance metrics in favor of volume measures), but the implementation is per-step CEM sampling, not multi-agent training. Adaptive diversity weight from online learning is also computationally heavier for real-time CEM.
mapping.source_context: Continuous control benchmarks (MuJoCo); population of N independent policies trained in parallel; behavioral embeddings from rollout state-action sequences; gradient-based (TD3) and evolution strategies (ES) instantiations of DvD.

confidence_components.notes: NeurIPS spotlight; solid theory and empirical results. Transfer risk moderate: population vs single-agent architecture; REE's CEM is a within-episode candidate generator, not a multi-agent population. The diversity metric insight (volume > pairwise distance) transfers cleanly.
